title: toxicodendron angustifolium
intro: Toxicodendron angustifolium, also known as poison oak, is a perennial plant in the Anacardiaceae family that is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat conditions like arthritis and eczema. The leaves and roots of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas, salves, and infusions, although caution is advised due to the plant's urushiol oil, which can cause skin irritation in some individuals.
